papers.title: Uneven Evolution of Cognition Across Generations of Generative AI Models
papers.reason: The paper evaluates cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than cognition in human participants.

papers.title: ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: The study addresses human dementia detection using speech spectrogram classifiers but includes no interactive behavioral analysis, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, or cognitive process model.

papers.title: Demystifying Video Reasoning
papers.reason: The paper studies reasoning and working memory inside diffusion-based video generation models rather than human cognition.

papers.title: The Richest Paradigm You're Not Using: Commercial Videogames at the Intersection of Human-Computer Interaction and Cognitive Science
papers.reason: The perspective centrally proposes interactive commercial games with behavioral timing and human eye tracking for studying attention and executive function, although it reports no empirical validation.

papers.title: Pupillometry and Brain Dynamics for Cognitive Load in Working Memory
papers.reason: The study analyzes pupil dynamics and responses during an interactive digit-span working-memory task, while its EEG Catch-22 classification does not establish qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Quant VideoGen: Auto-Regressive Long Video Generation via 2-Bit KV-Cache Quantization
papers.reason: The paper concerns KV-cache memory and autoregressive video generation rather than human participants or human cognition.

papers.title: Cognitive Load Estimation Using Brain Foundation Models and Interpretability for BCIs
papers.reason: The paper targets human cognitive-load monitoring from EEG but reports foundation-model classification and regional attribution without an interactive task methodology or qualifying spectral or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Computational Model of Brain-Heart Interactions via HRV and EEG Feature
papers.reason: The study uses data from an active working-memory task, but EEG is represented by band-power and Catch-22 features and the synthetic HRV model is not a generative cognitive process model.

papers.title: Unveiling the Heart-Brain Connection: An Analysis of ECG in Cognitive Performance
papers.reason: The methodology includes an active working-memory paradigm, but its EEG contribution is limited to spectral and Catch-22 features used as regression targets rather than detailed frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Your Eyes Controlled the Game: Real-Time Cognitive Training Adaptation based on Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data in Virtual Reality
papers.reason: Human participants perform adaptive sustained-attention and working-memory tasks in VR while eye tracking is centrally analyzed to control difficulty in real time.

papers.title: How Light Shapes Memory: Beta Synchrony in the Temporal-Parietal Cortex Predicts Cognitive Ergonomics for BCI Applications
papers.reason: The study combines interactive recall and sequence working-memory tasks with central EEG beta-phase connectivity and regional synchrony analysis.

papers.title: Setting up for failure: automatic discovery of the neural mechanisms of cognitive errors
papers.reason: The reported neural validation uses macaque data and therefore does not satisfy the requirement that the study population be human participants.

papers.title: A Quantitative Framework for Assessing Sleep Quality from EEG Time Series in Complex Dynamic Systems
papers.reason: The study includes a working-memory task with reaction-time measurement and centrally analyzes EEG phase-amplitude coupling, spectral power, and connectivity across cognitive states.

papers.title: Quantifying the Effects of Word Length, Frequency, and Predictability on Dyslexia
papers.reason: The paper centrally analyzes human eye-tracking measures during naturalistic reading but does not use an interactive decision task, EEG, or a qualifying generative cognitive model.

papers.title: Working Memory Functional Connectivity Analysis for Dementia Classification using EEG
papers.reason: The study combines staged working-memory tasks with detailed EEG functional-connectivity analysis using CPTE, phase-lag index, and graph metrics for AD and MCI classification.

papers.title: Bayesian Distributional Models of Executive Functioning
papers.reason: The evaluation uses controlled simulations and synthetically sampled sessions rather than a study population of human participants.

papers.title: Bibliometric-enhanced Systematic Literature Review of EEG in Education: Learning Concepts, Computational Methods, and Research Opportunities
papers.reason: This review concerns human EEG-based assessment of attention and learning but does not itself implement any of the four methodological elements.

papers.title: EEG-Based Framework for Reflexive and Perceptual Assessment in CLIS: Preliminary Study in Healthy Volunteers
papers.reason: The healthy-volunteer study centrally measures frequency-tagged SSAEP and SSVEP EEG responses, but its reflexive and perceptual paradigms are passive rather than interactive.

papers.title: EEG-Based Cognitive Load Classification During Landmark-Based VR Navigation
papers.reason: Participants actively navigate landmark-based VR routes, but the summary provides classification results without qualifying EEG frequency-domain or connectivity methodology.

papers.title: A Unified Cortical Circuit Model with Divisive Normalization and Self-Excitation for Robust Representation and Memory Maintenance
papers.reason: The paper presents a theoretical neural-circuit model without a human participant study or validation on human behavioral data.

papers.title: Dissociating Cognitive Load and Stress Responses Using Single-Channel EEG: Behavioral and Neural Correlates of Anxiety Across Cognitive States
papers.reason: The study assesses human cognitive load and stress, but the auditory protocol is not described as an interactive decision task and EEG analysis is limited to band-level and machine-learned features.

papers.title: Sleep Brain and Cardiac Activity Predict Cognitive Flexibility and Conceptual Reasoning Using Deep Learning
papers.reason: The paper predicts human executive-function test categories from passive sleep EEG and ECG, using band-power features and deep learning rather than task behavior or a cognitive process model.

papers.title: Evaluating Cognitive Assessment Tools:A Comparative Analysis of MMSE, RUDAS, SAGE, ADAS and MoCA for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: The review is relevant to human dementia assessment but does not implement behavioral trajectory analysis, eye tracking, EEG analysis, or computational cognitive modelling.

papers.title: Early Detection of Cognitive Impairment in Elderly using a Passive FPVS-EEG BCI and Machine Learning -- Extended Version
papers.reason: The paper targets early cognitive-impairment detection with passive FPVS-EEG, but it explicitly lacks behavioral interaction and reports a CNN approach without detailed qualifying spectral or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Immersive Virtual Reality Assessments of Working Memory and Psychomotor Skills: A Comparison between Immersive and Non-Immersive Assessments
papers.reason: Participants complete interactive digit-span, Corsi-block, and reaction-time tasks in VR and on PC, but no eye tracking, EEG, or cognitive process model is included.

papers.title: Triangulating LLM Progress through Benchmarks, Games, and Cognitive Tests
papers.reason: The study evaluates cognitive abilities and executive functions in LLMs rather than human participants.

papers.title: Interactive Virtual Games: Winners for Deep Cognitive Assessment
papers.reason: A clinical human sample completes interactive executive-function minigames with continuous behavioral, movement, and gaze trajectories used to characterize individual cognitive strategies.

papers.title: Mazed and Confused: A Dataset of Cybersickness, Working Memory, Mental Load, Physical Load, and Attention During a Real Walking Task in VR
papers.reason: Participants navigate mazes and perform attention and working-memory tasks while eye movements and continuous behavioral trajectories are recorded and analyzed.

papers.title: Pathformer3D: A 3D Scanpath Transformer for 360° Images
papers.reason: The model is evaluated on human panoramic eye-tracking datasets, but the viewing condition is passive and the Transformer is not a generative cognitive process model.

papers.title: Transcranial low-level laser stimulation in near infrared-II region for brain safety and protection
papers.reason: The human safety study includes EEG and executive-function assessments only as broad outcome measures without interactive-task details or qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.
